expect: Excluding test modules from the audit substrate will reduce isolated tools by >=15 and write-only tools by >=10 while low-truth tools remain within 0-2.
actual: Default production-only audit excludes 24 test modules. Compared with --include-tests, isolated tools fell from 58 to 47 (-11, -19.0%), write-only tools stayed flat at 27, and low-truth tools stayed flat at 1. Mean R-score rose from 0.443 to 0.489 and mean integration rose from 0.487 to 0.528.
diff: Expectation partially falsified. Test-module contamination was real and inflated the isolated count, but the reduction was 11 rather than the expected >=15 and write-only debt did not improve. The low-truth signal remained stable, indicating the substrate hardening removed noise without masking the main integration debt.
